title: The Hangover That Won't Lift
bullet1: A poem about love as intoxication
bullet2: When the mind and heart stop agreeing

title: It starts quietly
bullet2: The next, someone's laugh lives rent-free in your head
bullet3: The part of your brain that runs the show has quietly handed over the keys

title: The poem opens
bullet1: Love has taken away all my senses / like that of alcohol
bullet2: effects of booze reside for a short period
bullet3: but your intoxication doesn't want to go away

title: What makes it stay
bullet2: But twinkling eyes, ebony hair — that's a face. A real one.
bullet3: Specificity is what the mind cannot release

title: When love feels like physics
bullet1: like a moth attracted to the flame
bullet3: like cold to the winter / like a rainbow after the rain

title: The collision
bullet1: Earlier, mind and heart worked in sync
bullet3: No ground to stand on. No sky to cover my head with.

title: Not a malfunction
bullet1: When the system is overturned, that's a signal
bullet2: The only honest one you'll get that something actually matters
bullet3: That's not a problem to solve. That's the experience itself.
